meaning: adj.辉耀的,华丽的,火焰似的,艳丽的
examples: Critics attack his lavish spending and flamboyant style.
Freddie Mercury was a flamboyant star of the hard rock scene.
Unlike the general run of peasantry they had a taste for flamboyant dress and gesture; but they usually shared the peasants' religious beliefs and superstitions.
Bucksbaum has staunchly resisted paying himself the outsize salaries that more flamboyant REIT managers collect.
Many fashionable and flamboyant people, including the Maharajah of Mysore, had glamorized the product.
